commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And they answered him, He was a hairy man, and wearing a belt of leather about his loins. And he said, It is Elijah the Tishbite. Hebrew ish bahal sehar, "a man lord of hair "or all covered with it, having a long beard, like the ancient sages, (St. Jerome, in Ezechiel xxxv. ) and clothed with a skin, (Bo chart) as the first inhabitants of the earth, the heroes, prophets and St. John the Baptist, are described, Hebrews xi. , and Matthew iii. So Statius (ii. ) says: Tiresiæ vultus, voces et vellera nota Induitur.
